cycle.note: Locked: the 1974 low's date/price is undisputed historical fact (real data + independent confirming sources, see module docstring). The '(IV)' WAVE LABEL itself, however, is a cross-market-inherited framing (Pring's principle of commonality), not independently verified for the Nasdaq specifically -- disclosed in each label's own label_caveat, not silently presented as equally certain as ^DJI's citation-backed count.

labels.granularity_note: value is a CLOSE, not a true intraday extreme -- this source only carries real intraday OHLC from 1984-10-11 onward; earlier rows repeat the close into o/h/l
labels.date_discrepancy_note: this source's actual extreme in the search window falls on 1974-10-03, not the commonly-cited 1974-09-30 -- reported as found, not silently corrected to the textbook date
labels.label_caveat: Labeled '(IV)' for consistency with ^GSPC's/^DJI's own notation, but this is a CROSS-MARKET-INHERITED framing, not an independently verified Nasdaq-specific wave count -- see module docstring. The Nasdaq's own recorded history (since 1971-02-05) is too short by this date to resolve a preceding wave (I)-(II)-(III) on its own.

labels.granularity_note: value is a CLOSE, not a true intraday extreme -- this source only carries real intraday OHLC from 1984-10-11 onward; earlier rows repeat the close into o/h/l
labels.note: start of (V) = the (IV) trough, same locked convention as ^GSPC's/^DJI's own (V) anchors (dashboard_build_spec.md §9)

primary_label.description: Project-derived hypothesis: a single Primary-degree advance in progress since the 1974 Cycle-equivalent (IV) trough, per the Cycle (V) framing above. NOT subdivided into Primary I-V internal waves here -- same reasoning as ^GSPC's/^DJI's own Primary/Supercycle primary_label: the only pivot detector available ('slow' scale) does not resolve multi-decade structure distinctly from intermediate-scale structure. Left for human review on the deep-history panel.

alternates.label: Multiple independent sources (Nasdaq.com market-history summary, general 1973-74 crash retrospectives) confirming the Nasdaq's own 1974 bear-market low, variously described as a 'bottom in September 1974' with the index down 59% from its 1973 peak, or a 35% decline in 1974 alone
alternates.caveat: General historical confirmation of the crash/low's severity and approximate timing, not an Elliott Wave degree claim -- the wave framing itself (Cycle-equivalent, by cross-market commonality) is project-derived, see module docstring.

alternates.label: Lewis Jones, ElliottWave-Forecast.com: QQQ's own long-term structure (from its March 1999 inception, NOT the Nasdaq Composite's full history) treats the March 2000 high, October 2002 low, and November 2008 low as significant cycle-scale turning points
alternates.caveat: Covers only QQQ's own 1999+ history, not the full 1971+ ^IXIC range this bootstrap addresses -- included as a directly-cited, lower-degree data point for FUTURE confluence checking against post-2000 structure, same role ^GSPC's own Primary-degree alternate plays there. Not evidence for the 1974 anchor itself.

invalidation.basis: A confirmed daily close below the real 1974 low would invalidate the Cycle (V)/Primary-in-progress hypothesis.

note: QQQ has no independent bootstrap; it mechanically inherits ^IXIC's cycle-degree anchor (1974) per dashboard_build_spec.md §2/§8, regenerated every build run and never hand-edited. That anchor predates QQQ's own history (starts 1999-03-10), so there is nothing to substitute this phase -- own_anchor_substitutions is empty by construction, not omitted.
